target_spec: MCP SEP-3004 — Tamper-Evident Audit Record Contract (modelcontextprotocol/modelcontextprotocol#3004)
description: Compat vectors reproducing SEP-3004's own published known-answer digest, joining it to action_ref (docs/spec/action-ref.md) for cross-producer identity, and applying anchoring-precedence-ref-v1's existence/precedence split to SEP-3004's own reserved-and-deferred external-anchoring boundary (§2.8).

vectors.description: SEP-3004 Conformance section, C-REC-3 fixed known-answer test. Single segment-head record (previous_hash: null) carrying both caller-governance and runtime-security extensions.
vectors.note: Published by SEP-3004 as its two-extension known-answer digest. Reproduced here byte-for-byte with a runner independent of the SEP's reference implementation (GIF, notboatanchor/gif) and of Interlock's runtime-security implementation.

vectors.description: Same tool_call event as the single-extension form (caller-governance only), with both event_hash (SEP-3004 chain-scoped identity) and action_ref (docs/spec/action-ref.md cross-producer identity) computed from its own fields.
vectors.note: event_hash matches SEP-3004's own stated single-extension digest (d494769c...), confirming this fixture against the SEP text independently of the two-extension vector. action_ref is the identity SEP-3004's protected core does not define: event_id (§2.1) is an opaque id unique 'within the chain', not independently recomputable by a party without chain access.

vectors.description: Identical event, occurred_at drifted by 1ms. Same principal, tool, outcome.
vectors.note: Both identifiers move together under the same 1ms drift -- action_ref carries the same field sensitivity as SEP-3004's own chain, expressed as a value computable by a second producer with no access to the chain.

vectors.description: External anchor for the known-answer chain head exists but is timestamped AFTER the event's own occurred_at -- SEP-3004 §2.8 reserves and defers exactly this boundary ('External Anchoring... addresses a distinct, weaker-priority threat... orthogonal to the within-system chain construction').
vectors.note: A verifier that only checks SEP-3004's own chain integrity (§2.4-2.6) cannot see this gap -- the chain hashes correctly either way. Same existence-vs-precedence split as ../anchoring-precedence-ref/, applied to a SEP-3004 chain head instead of a Mycelium trail.

vectors.description: External anchor for the same chain head, strictly BEFORE occurred_at.
